top_stories.title: SailPoint、Entro買収の意向を発表、Agentic Fabricを加速・強化し、AI主導型企業の未来に向けたセキュリティを強化
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has announced its intent to acquire Entro Security, aiming to accelerate and enhance its Agentic Fabric capabilities for AI-driven enterprises. The acquisition is intended to strengthen security for non-human identities and secrets management within SailPoint's identity security platform.

top_stories.title: SailPoint completes acquisition of Entro Security to secure agentic identities
top_stories.summary: SailPoint has completed its acquisition of Tel Aviv-based Entro Security. The deal expands SailPoint's identity governance capabilities to include securing agentic identities, secrets, and non-human identities.

top_stories.title: Omada Agent Governance, Extends Identity Governance to AI Agents
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched Omada Agent Governance, a new solution designed to extend identity governance capabilities to AI agents. The product enables organizations to discover AI agents, establish accountability, manage their access, and reduce risk across cloud environments.

top_stories.title: Introduces AI-Powered Agentic Acceleration for Identity Security Modernization
top_stories.summary: SailPoint introduced SailPoint Agentic Acceleration on June 16, an AI-powered methodology designed to accelerate enterprise migration from legacy on-premises identity systems to modern platforms. The offering targets identity security modernization and represents a new product/methodology launch from a major IGA vendor.

top_stories.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
top_stories.summary: A malware strain called 'Djinn' stealer is targeting cloud and AI credentials by exploiting CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp. The infostealer specifically targets credentials connecting development and admin environments to broader enterprise systems.

news.title: Azure CLI Password Spray Hits at Least 78 Microsoft Accounts in 81M+ Attempts
news.summary: A large-scale automated password spray attack targeting Microsoft Azure CLI has compromised at least 78 accounts across more than 81 million attempts between June 12 and June 26. The attack originates from an IPv6 address range controlled by infrastructure provider LSHIY LLC and was identified by Huntress researchers.

news.title: Microsoft accelerates quantum-safe roadmap as risks grow
news.summary: Microsoft announced it is accelerating its quantum-safe security roadmap in response to faster-than-expected advances in quantum computing. The initiative involves transitioning away from current encryption standards, which directly impacts authentication and identity security protocols used across IAM systems.

news.title: Microsoft Warns Poisoned MCP Tool Descriptions Can Make AI Agents Leak Data
news.summary: Microsoft research demonstrates how attackers can poison MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool descriptions to manipulate AI agents into exfiltrating company data without triggering security alerts. The research, from Microsoft Incident Response, highlights authorization and access control risks in AI agent frameworks that act on behalf of users.

news.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
news.summary: Security firm LayerX demonstrated a technique called "BioShocking" that tricks AI browsers and assistants into extracting and exfiltrating user credentials by convincing them they are playing a game. The attack successfully targeted six AI browsers and assistants including OpenAI's ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity's Comet, and Anthropic's Claude browser extension.

news.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
news.summary: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-46817, CVSS 9.8) in Oracle E-Business Suite's Payments module involving improper privilege management and authentication flaws is being actively exploited in the wild. The flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to potentially take over susceptible Oracle EBS instances.

news.title: NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
news.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion group breached the NAIC's systems by exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in an Oracle PeopleSoft server. The NAIC states that only publicly available data, outdated logs, and configuration files were stolen in the attack.

news.title: Why Post-Quantum Cryptography Starts With Credentials
news.summary: The article discusses the threat quantum computing poses to current public-key cryptography protecting credentials and authentication systems. It focuses on the need for organizations to transition to post-quantum cryptography to protect credentials before quantum hardware becomes capable of breaking RSA and elliptic curve cryptography.

news.title: JumpServer: Open-Source Privileged Access Management
news.summary: JumpServer is an open-source Privileged Access Management platform designed for DevOps and IT teams. It provides secure access management for SSH, RDP, Kubernetes, Database, and RemoteApp endpoints.

news.title: Agentic AI Has an Identity Problem and Attackers Know It
news.summary: Token Security has published analysis identifying agentic AI systems as a growing privileged identity security risk, noting that AI agents can access data, trigger workflows, and take actions across enterprise systems. The piece focuses on the need to govern AI agent identities as a form of privileged access management within enterprise security frameworks.

news.title: CyberFOX Acquires Timus Networks, Adding SASE with Zero Trust Network Access to its Cybersecurity Platform
news.summary: CyberFOX has acquired Timus Networks to add SASE and Zero Trust Network Access (ZTNA) capabilities to its cybersecurity platform. The acquisition extends CyberFOX's portfolio with secure connectivity, secure access, secure web gateway, and adaptive policy controls.

news.title: Cerby’s Growth Accelerates as Enterprises Move to Secure Disconnected Applications at Scale
news.summary: Cerby, a platform focused on securing disconnected and unmanageable applications, reported 130% year-over-year ARR growth along with new customer wins and executive hires. The company operates in the IAM ecosystem, addressing access management for applications that don't support standard identity protocols.

news.title: How IAM providers are preparing for agentic AI
news.summary: IAM providers are developing strategies and solutions to secure agentic AI systems as enterprises prepare for widespread deployment. The article examines how identity and access management vendors are approaching the challenge of authenticating and authorizing autonomous AI agents.

news.title: Password Management Market to Reach $42.21 Billion by 2035 as Cyberattacks and Identity Security Risks Drive Enterprise Adoption | Report by SNS Insider
news.summary: SNS Insider projects the global password management market will reach $42.21 billion by 2035, with the U.S. accounting for $12.53 billion and Europe $2.63 billion. Growth drivers cited include rising cybercrime losses, GDPR compliance requirements, remote work expansion, and increasing MFA adoption.

news.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials
news.summary: Russia's FSB conducted a long-running campaign using fake support texts to steal messaging account credentials from government officials, military personnel, politicians, and activists across Ukraine, Europe, and the U.S. The operation was uncovered jointly by the SSU and FBI, targeting authentication credentials to gain unauthorized access to messaging accounts.

news.title: Windows 11 beta build enhances smart card security for remote sessions
news.summary: Microsoft released a new Windows 11 Insider Beta build that adds smart card security enhancements for remote desktop environments. Administrators can configure Azure Virtual Desktop and Windows 365 sessions to automatically disconnect when a smart card is redirected or removed during a session.

news.title: Cisco Adds NHI to Security Stack With Astrix, WideField Acquisitions
news.summary: Cisco has acquired Astrix and WideField to add non-human identity (NHI) security capabilities to its security stack. The acquisitions position Cisco to address machine and service account identity governance as agentic AI workloads increase demand for NHI management solutions.

news.title: Truist Securities Reiterates a Buy on SailPoint (SAIL), Keeps the PT
news.summary: Truist Securities reiterated a Buy rating on SailPoint (SAIL) stock on June 17, maintaining its price target. The update is a routine analyst rating affirmation for the identity governance vendor.

news.title: Robinhood Cuts Access Approval Time to Support High-Velocity Development
news.summary: Robinhood's engineering security team reengineered its system access approval process to reduce approval time while maintaining security for developers. The initiative focused on streamlining access management workflows to support high-velocity software development environments.

news.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
news.summary: Two members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group pleaded guilty in the UK to charges related to an August 2024 cyberattack on Transport for London. Scattered Spider is known for social engineering and identity-based attacks, including SIM swapping and MFA bypass techniques used to compromise large organizations.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, successfully compromising high-profile accounts including the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI bot to bypass normal authentication controls were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a data breach that CISA is still working to contain. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers while CISA works to invalidate the leaked credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential and secrets management failure.
